task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named DataSyncService.kt open in the editor, containing 'class DataSyncService : Service()'.
Look for code creating a NotificationChannel with arguments 'data_sync_channel', 'Data Sync', and NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_LOW (or value 2).

task_completion.description: Call startForeground with correct notification details
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a call to startForeground(1, notification) where the notification builder sets .setContentTitle("Syncing Data") and .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_popup_sync).

task_completion.description: Implement onStartCommand and onBind with correct return values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'return START_STICKY' inside the onStartCommand method and 'return null' inside the onBind method.

task_completion.description: Add required foreground service permissions to AndroidManifest.xml
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for AndroidManifest.xml open in the editor showing <uses-permission> tags for both 'android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE' and 'android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC'.

task_completion.description: Declare the service in AndroidManifest.xml with correct attributes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a <service> tag for DataSyncService containing attributes android:foregroundServiceType="dataSync" and android:exported="false".
Look for a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message in the terminal or Android Studio Build tool window.

integrity.description: Agent used an IDE or text editor to modify the files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Android Studio or another text editor being used to edit the Kotlin and XML files, rather than just terminal commands echoing text.
The build success message should appear in a genuine terminal or IDE build window, showing actual Gradle task execution logs.
